mappings.rationaleAI: Comprehensive Islam addresses Muhammad (A.2), early expansion (A.3), Middle Ages (A.5), modern (A.6), today (A.7). Sharia relates to law (B.3, C.3.d). Sunni (C.2.c) and Shia (C.2.d) are covered. Doctrines of God relate to C.1.a.
The Quran addresses B.1 and doctrines of prophecy (C.1.e).
Muhammad addresses origin of Islam (A.2) and prophecy (C.1.e).
Islamic history addresses foundations (A.3), Abbasids (A.4), Middle Ages (A.5), and sacred places (C.3.b).
Islamic law addresses Shari'ah (B.3, C.3.d).
Global Islam addresses modern Islam (A.6) and Islam today (A.7).
Philosophy in Islamic lands addresses Islamic theology and philosophy (B.4).
Avicenna stands as one of the most important Islamic philosophers, making this text essential for 828.B.4 on major schools of Islamic philosophy.
Fundamentalism relates to modern Islam (A.6) and Sunnism variations (C.2.c).
Islamic spirituality relates to Sufism (B.5).
Pilgrimage to Mecca addresses the Five Pillars (C.3.a).
Conceptions of God relate to Islamic doctrines (C.1.a).
Islam as Abrahamic tradition relates to history (A) and God (C.1.a).
Theology relates to Islamic philosophy (B.4) and beliefs (C.1).
Iran's Islamic Revolution relates to modern Islam (A.6).
Crusades relate to Middle Ages of Islam (A.5).
Addresses the conflict including the Dome of the Rock and Jerusalem's significance in Islam, relevant to 828.A.7 on Islam today.
War and religion relate to social and ethical doctrines (C.1.g).
Hermeneutics relates to Qur'anic interpretation (B.1).
Blasphemy relates to social doctrines (C.1.g).
Secularism relates to modern Islam (A.6).
Silk Road relates to Abbasid developments (A.4) and Middle Ages (A.5).
Pakistan relates to Islam today (A.7).
Southeast Asian Islam relates to modern Islam (A.6).
Islam in Africa relates to Middle Ages of Islam (A.5).
Byzantine-Islamic interactions relate to early Abbasid period (A.4).
Ritual relates to Islamic practices (C.3).
Addresses faith's relationship to reason including contrasts with Islam's emphasis on divine law and obedience, relevant to Islamic doctrines concerning God and social and ethical doctrines.
Covers monasticism including geographic distribution and communal organization of religious orders, relevant to variations among urban and rustic Sufi orders.
